_note: GTH-V15-25 step 1 ONLY: the reposix-arm (pure-git/POSIX) agent command trajectory, extracted verbatim from a live captured benchmark session JSONL. This is the canonical-trajectory fixture for the GitHub backend row. Step 2 (the CI byte-guard verifier that replays this against an ephemeral sim) is NOT implemented here. No secrets, no backend body content.
task: read 3 issues (#56,#57,#60), edit 1 (#60 body marker), commit, push
agent_commands: echo "=== issues/56.md ==="; cat issues/56.md; echo; echo "=== issues/57.md ==="; cat issues/57.md; echo; echo "=== issues/60.md ==="; cat issues/60.md

printf '<!-- reposix-bench-marker: reposix-run1 -->\n' >> issues/60.md
tail -5 issues/60.md
git add issues/60.md && git commit -m "bench: set marker reposix-run1 on issue 60"

provenance.note: push step returns the documented read-only-cut error (reposix-github create/update/delete are read-only in this build cut, crates/reposix-github/src/lib.rs); the read+edit+commit+push-attempt trajectory is intact and replayable.
